title: CSPG: Core Idea
content: • Random partitioning of dataset [公式:D] into groups • Each group shares routing vectors (red) • Build sparser, smaller graphs for each partition • [公式:G_1] (left) and [公式:G_2] (right) in example 5mm Key Benefits: • Sparse graphs allow larger steps for fast approach • Routing vectors enable cross-partition navigation • Two-stage search: fast approaching + precise search

title: Two-Stage Search Algorithm
content: Stage 1: Fast Approaching • Start from routing vectors in one partition • Navigate sparse graph [公式:G_i] toward query [公式:q] • Stop when distance stops decreasing significantly • Output: candidate set [公式:C_i] from partition [公式:i] 5mm Stage 2: Precise Search • For each candidate [公式:c C_i], examine its routing neighbors • These neighbors belong to other partitions • Expand search across partitions using routing connections • Final result: nearest neighbors from entire dataset 5mm Algorithm Complexity: • Stage 1: [公式:O( n)] distance computations • Stage 2: [公式:O(k m)] where [公式:k] is candidate size, [公式:m] is routing degree • Total: Much fewer than exhaustive search

title: Summary: CSPG Advantages
content: • Efficiency: Sparse graphs reduce distance computations • Scalability: Partitioning enables parallel processing • Accuracy: Cross-partition expansion ensures high recall • Flexibility: Works with various graph construction methods 5mm Key Innovations: • Random partitioning with shared routing vectors • Two-stage search: fast approaching + precise expansion • Sparse graphs for large-step navigation • Cross-partition connectivity via routing neighbors 5mm Next: We'll evaluate CSPG against state-of-the-art methods on benchmark datasets.

title: Conclusion: Key Contributions of CSPG
content: • Novel Partitioning Strategy: CSPG randomly partitions datasets to create smaller, sparser proximity graphs • Efficient Two-Stage Search: Combines fast proximity search with cross-partition precise expansion • Balanced Performance: Achieves optimal trade-off between speed (QPS) and accuracy (Recall@10) • Practical Improvements: Demonstrates consistent performance gains across multiple datasets and graph algorithms 0.5cm Core Innovation: Partitioning reduces distance computations while maintaining search quality through intelligent routing vector utilization

title: Experimental Validation: Superior Performance
content: • CSPG achieves higher QPS \& Recall@10 than traditional proximity graphs • 1.5x speedup for Vamana and HCNNG across all datasets • Pushes Pareto frontier outward: Better speed without sacrificing accuracy

title: Conclusion: CSPG Achieves Superior Performance
content: Key Contributions: • Novel partitioning strategy creates smaller, sparser graphs • Efficient two-stage search: fast proximity + cross-partition expansion • Balances speed (QPS) and accuracy (Recall@10) 0.3cm Experimental Results: • Higher QPS \& Recall@10 than traditional proximity graphs • 1.5x speedup for Vamana and HCNNG • Validates partitioning reduces computations while maintaining quality
